FIRST AVENUE, EVANSVILLE IN 47710 Phone: 812-647-9498 DRUG SCR, URINE PAIN, REFLEX DEFINIT Complete this on or around 5/20/2026. Today's medication changes Pick up these medications at Walmart Neighborhood Market 5452 - EVANSVILLE, IN - 2500 N. FIRST AVENUE morphine Labs ordered today Instructions • • • • • • • You saw Mary Kasel, NP on Wednesday May 20, 2026. The following issues were addressed: COPD, end-stage Palliative care patient Dyspnea, unspecified type Long term (current) use of opiate analgesic Acute bronchitis, unspecified organism COPD, very severe COPD, frequent exacerbations Before your next appointment, you can verify your information before you arrive. Up to one weeks prior to your appointment, you can review and make changes to your contact information, insurance, or health history information directly in MyChart, saving you time at registration. Simply click on your upcoming appointment in MyChart to get started. Once you arrive for your appointment, just check in at the front desk. MyChart Today's Visit

Look for the big dark gray awning labeled Deaconess Critical Care. Located on the first floor of the detached parking garage. Look for the big dark gray awning labeled Deaconess Critical Care. Enter Deaconess Midtown lobby. Take a left and then a right, walk down the hallway (cafeteria will be on your left). Check in at Patient Registration using the kiosk or at the desk. What's Next

Accu-Chek Guide Me w/Device Kit Indications: Diabetes Provide per pt preference or as covered by insurance Check blood glucose levels once a day as ordered per Provider. DXE11.9 Accu-Chek Guide Test strip Generic drug: glucose blood VI test Please check blood sugars once a day per orders Accu-Chek Softclix Lancets Misc Indications: Diabetes Provide per pt preference or as covered by insurance Check blood glucose levels once a day as ordered per Provider. DXE11.9 acetaminophen 500 MG tablet Commonly known as: TYLENOL * albuterol 108 (90 Base) MCG/ACT inhaler Inhale 2 puffs by mouth every 6 (six) hours if needed for Wheezing or Shortness of Breath * albuterol (2.5 MG/3ML) 0.083% nebulizer solution Commonly known as: PROVENTIL Take 1 vial (2.5 mg) by nebulization every 6 (six) hours if needed for Wheezing or Shortness of Breath Indications: Reversible Disease of Blockage in Breathing Passages, Spasm of Lung Air Passages arformoterol 15 MCG/2ML nebulizer solution Commonly known as: BROVANA Take 2 mL (15 mcg) by nebulization 2 times daily aspirin 81 MG chewable tablet atorvaSTATin 20 MG tablet Commonly known as: LIPITOR Take 1 tablet (20 mg) by mouth daily azithromycin 250 MG tablet Commonly known as: ZITHROMAX TAKE 1 TABLET BY MOUTH EVERY MONDAY WEDNESDAY AND FRIDAY FOR LONG TERM RECURRENT EXACERBATIONS OF OBSTRUCTIVE LUNG DISEASE budesonide 0.5 MG/2ML nebulizer suspension Commonly known as: PULMICORT Take 2 mL (500 mcg) by nebulization 2 times daily Dupixent 300 MG/2ML pen Generic drug: dupilumab Inject 2 mL (300 mg) into the skin every 14 days GNP Alcohol Swabs 70 % Pads Indications: Diabetes Provide per pt preference or as covered by insurance. For use to monitor blood glucose levels as ordered per Provider. DXE11.9 guaiFENesin 100 MG/5ML soln Commonly known as: ROBITUSSIN Take 10 mL (200 mg) by mouth every 4 hours (while awake) for 861 doses ipratropium 0.06 % nasal spray Commonly known as: ATROVENT 2 sprays by Nasal route 3 times daily Your Medication List as of May 20, 2026  4:20 PMYour Medication List

# Page 4^page=4
Lisa A. English (CSN: 853759584) • Printed at 5/20/2026  4:20 PM Page 4 of 5   Your Medication List (continued) as of May 20, 2026  4:20 PM ipratropium-albuterol 0.5-2.5 (3) MG/3ML nebulizer solution Commonly known as: DUO-NEB Take 3 mL by nebulization every 6 (six) hours if needed lisinopril-hydrochlorothiazide 20-25 MG per tablet Commonly known as: PRINZIDE, ZESTORETIC Take 1 tablet by mouth daily Do not start before April 22, 2026. metFORMIN 500 MG tablet Commonly known as: GLUCOPHAGE Take 1 tablet (500 mg) by mouth 2 times daily (with meals) metoprolol succinate 50 MG XL tablet Commonly known as: TOPROL XL Take 1 tablet (50 mg) by mouth 2 times daily morphine 10 MG/5ML solution Started by: Mary Kasel, NP Take 2.5 mL (5 mg) by mouth every 6 (six) hours if needed for Pain (max 4 doses per day) Indications: Difficulty Breathing Replaces: morphine 20 MG/ML concentrated solution MYLANTA PO naloxone 4 MG/0.1ML nasal spray Commonly known as: Narcan Administer 4 mg (one nasal spray) to one nostril. If needed may administer additonal one spray to opposite nostril after 2-3 minutes. omeprazole 40 MG capsule Commonly known as: PRILOSEC Take 1 capsule (40 mg) by mouth 1 (one) time each day Indications: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease ondansetron 8 MG disintegrating tablet Commonly known as: ZOFRAN ODT Take 1 tablet (8 mg) by mouth every 8 (eight) hours if needed for Nausea predniSONE 10 MG tablet Commonly known as: DELTASONE Take 4 tablets by mouth daily x1 day, THEN 3 tabs daily x3 days, THEN 2 tabs daily x7 days, THEN 1.5 tabs daily x10 days, THEN 1 tab daily x21 days. Start taking on: May 6, 2026 sulfamethoxazole-trimethoprim 800-160 MG per tablet Commonly known as: BACTRIM DS Take 1 tablet by mouth three times a week for 75 doses THEOPHYLLINE PO Yupelri 175 MCG/3ML nebulizer solution Generic drug: revefenacin Take 3 mL (175 mcg) by nebulization daily  very important  * This list has 2 medication(s) that are the same as other medications prescribed for you. Read the directions carefully, and ask your doctor or other care provider to review them with you.

# Page 5^page=5
Lisa A. English (CSN: 853759584) • Printed at 5/20/2026  4:20 PM Page 5 of 5       Did you know that as a patient you can request an estimate for the cost of non-emergent services?  Go to Deaconess.com/pricing, e-mail DeaconessCostQuote@deaconess.com, or call 812-450-COST (2678) for more details.  All estimates will be provided within five business days.          Patients can request an estimate of the costs for nonemergency health care services from their health care providers and the health facility. For an estimate, please contact 812-450-2678(COST) or email deaconesscostquote@deaconess.com. Patient Satisfaction If you receive a patient satisfaction survey, please tell us how we are doing. If you have concerns, comments or questions, please list those on the survey or feel free to contact us at: 812-450-3241. Additional Information Price Estimates Available!
